# [PROJECT_NAME]-shared — Claude Code Context (Shared Library)
# Generated by APEX · Seeded from eeik_bootstrap
# Replace ALL values in [BRACKETS] before committing.

## What this library is
A shared Maven library published to [Nexus / GitHub Packages / AWS CodeArtifact].
It provides: [list: common DTOs, utilities, security filters, audit logging, etc.]
Consumed by: [list services that depend on this library]

## Architecture rules for shared libraries
- **No Spring Boot auto-configuration** that enables itself without explicit opt-in
- **No transitive dependency pollution** — scope dependencies carefully (provided/optional where possible)
- **Backward compatibility** — follow semantic versioning strictly; breaking changes = major version bump
- **No business logic** — this library is infrastructure/cross-cutting only
- **No database access** — shared libs must not own a schema or data source

## Package structure
```
src/main/java/com.[company].shared/
  dto/          — common request/response DTOs
  exception/    — base exception classes (no catch logic)
  security/     — JWT utilities, security filters
  audit/        — audit logging annotations + AOP
  validation/   — custom JSR-380 validators
  util/         — pure utility classes (no Spring dependencies)
  config/       — @Configuration classes (opt-in via @Import only)
```

## Versioning
- Format: `[major].[minor].[patch]` (semver)
- Breaking API change → major bump, notify all consumers via Slack + PR
- New feature (backward-compatible) → minor bump
- Bug fix → patch bump
- Current version: [x.y.z]

## Publishing
- Registry: [Nexus URL / GitHub Packages]
- Published by: CI pipeline on merge to `main`
- Snapshot builds: `-SNAPSHOT` suffix on feature branches

## Standards (same as Spring Boot — apply strictly)
- Constructor injection only, all injected fields `final`
- `jakarta.*` only — no `javax.*`
- No `System.out.println()` — SLF4J parameterised logging
- All public API has Javadoc
- No hardcoded configuration values

## Test strategy
- JUnit 5 + AssertJ — no Spring context in unit tests (pure unit)
- Coverage: 90% minimum (higher bar for shared libs — consumers rely on them)
- No Testcontainers in shared lib tests — no DB access allowed

## Consumer onboarding
```xml
<!-- Add to consuming service pom.xml -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.[company]</groupId>
  <artifactId>[project-name]-shared</artifactId>
  <version>[x.y.z]</version>
</dependency>
```

## Key eeik_bootstrap agents to use
- `senior-developer` — API design decisions, backward compatibility review
- `java-tech-lead` — architectural decisions about what belongs in shared vs service
- `security-auditor` — security utility review (JWT, filters, validators)
- `java-tester` — comprehensive test coverage for shared utilities

## What Claude must NOT do in this repo
- Do not add Spring Boot starter auto-configuration without explicit opt-in mechanism
- Do not add business-domain logic (no Order, Customer, Payment concepts here)
- Do not add a data source, JPA entities, or Flyway/Liquibase migrations
- Do not introduce a breaking API change without a major version bump discussion
- Do not add dependencies with GPL or LGPL licences without legal review
